mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive 60-day return direction in > 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in > 55% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY, or the regime-conditional alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the inflation index rises above 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 4.0%, markets price in aggressive future tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of threshold-crossing events, or the predictive log-odds ratio falls below 1.1 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer demand, which directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01, or positive payroll surprises fail to predict positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates a shift from durable goods to services consumption that weakens capital expenditure cycles and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day return direction, or the signal accuracy drops below 48%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production during a monetary easing cycle signals improved capacity utilization and margin expansion that triggers immediate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 50% of instances when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% during easing periods, or the regime-specific alpha vanishes after 18 months.
